hotel.name: S.22 Hotel Suratthani
hotel.description: A modern budget hotel that stands out for including free breakfast — unusual at this price point in Surat Thani. Rooms are clean and functional with reliable air conditioning and good WiFi. The breakfast buffet is basic but useful for travelers with early ferry departures. Central location within easy walking distance of the Saturday Night Market and Tapi River.
highlights: Free breakfast included — valuable for early departure days
Modern, clean rooms at budget prices
Central location near markets and transport connections

similarHotels.name: Sleep Box Hostel Suratthani
similarHotels.description: The most popular budget option in Surat Thani, designed specifically for transit travelers who need a clean, central bed near the bus-ferry hub. Modern dorm pods with privacy curtains, individual reading lights, USB charging ports, and lockers. The location a few minutes' walk from Talad Kaset makes it ideal for early ferry departures — staff are well-versed in booking onward transport.
highlights: Walking distance to Talad Kaset bus-ferry hub
Modern pod-style dorms with privacy and USB charging
Knowledgeable staff for onward island connections

similarHotels.name: Rajthani Hotel
similarHotels.description: A well-run mid-budget hotel in central Surat Thani with consistently high reviews for cleanliness and value. Air-conditioned rooms with hot showers, complimentary WiFi, and free self-parking. Not fancy, but reliable — the kind of place where everything works and staff are helpful with travel arrangements. Walking distance to the main markets, riverside, and transport offices.
highlights: Consistent 8.6 review score for value and cleanliness
Central location within walking distance of markets and Tapi River

similarHotels.name: 100 Islands Resort & Spa
similarHotels.description: A surprising find at the budget end — a resort property with swimming pool, spa, and complimentary buffet breakfast at a price point that undercuts most Bangkok budget hotels. The name references the 100 islands dotting the bay visible from Surat Thani's coast. Rooms are comfortable and well-maintained. The pool is the key differentiator at this price, making it a good choice for longer stays.
highlights: Swimming pool — rare and valuable at this price in Surat Thani

aiContent.hookIntro: Booking S.22 Hotel Suratthani without knowing the breakfast is basic can leave early ferry travelers hungry and frustrated.
aiContent.intro: S.22 Hotel Suratthani offers clean, modern rooms with reliable air conditioning and WiFi at budget prices, plus a free basic breakfast that's handy for quick starts. It's ideal for budget travelers, couples, and families needing a central spot near markets and transport in Surat Thani.
whyBook: Free breakfast saves money on early ferry days
Clean rooms with reliable AC and WiFi
Central location walkable to Saturday Night Market
Functional setup for short transit stays
whyNotBook: Breakfast buffet is basic with limited variety
No pool or spa for relaxation after travel
Rooms are functional but lack luxury touches
Noise from central location during market nights
Early check-out may rush the included breakfast
aiContent.skipIf: Skip if you're seeking resort-style amenities like a pool in a transit-focused budget stay.
aiContent.detailedReview: Checking into S.22 Hotel Suratthani feels straightforward and efficient, with a modern lobby that sets a no-frills tone for budget travelers. Rooms deliver on cleanliness and functionality, featuring reliable air conditioning that keeps things cool in Surat Thani's humid climate, along with good WiFi speeds suitable for booking ferries or checking emails. The standout amenity is the free breakfast buffet, which includes basic items like toast, eggs, and coffee—useful for guests catching early departures to the islands, though it lacks variety for longer stays. Food quality sticks to simple Thai and Western staples, nothing gourmet but filling enough to start the day. Service is polite and helpful, focusing on quick check-ins for transit guests rather than personalized attention. Typical guests are budget-conscious couples or families using Surat Thani as a stopover, often heading to Koh Samui or other Gulf spots; you'll see backpackers and short-stay visitors milling about, drawn by the value in a central spot. Overall, it's a practical choice for those prioritizing location and basics over extras, with the free breakfast providing a rare perk at this price point in town.
aiContent.locationReality: S.22 Hotel Suratthani sits in the city center, within 5-10 minutes' walk of the Saturday Night Market and Tapi River waterfront. It's convenient for transport connections like the nearby bus station or ferry piers, reachable by a short Grab ride costing 100-200 THB. No major beaches nearby as Surat Thani is inland, so island getaways require a 30-60 minute drive to the pier plus ferry time.
localSignals: Walking distance to Saturday Night Market: 5-10 minutes
Reliable WiFi included in all rooms
Free breakfast buffet for early departures
Central to bus-ferry transport hubs: 5-15 minute Grab for 100 THB
aiContent.travellerNote: Guests often praise the clean rooms and free breakfast value but note the basic buffet lacks variety and the central spot can get noisy from nearby markets.
bookingTips: Request higher-floor rooms to avoid street noise
Book via Agoda for frequent Thailand discounts
Prices spike in December-January for island transit peak
Verify breakfast inclusion as policy for early check-outs
Opt for standard rooms if traveling light for transit

similarHotels.name: Sleep Box Hostel Suratthani
similarHotels.howDifferent: Cheaper pod-style beds for solo transit travelers, but no private rooms or breakfast like S.22.

similarHotels.name: Rajthani Hotel
similarHotels.howDifferent: Similar budget pricing with strong cleanliness focus, but lacks the free breakfast perk of S.22.

similarHotels.name: 100 Islands Resort & Spa
similarHotels.howDifferent: Offers pool and spa at slightly higher rates, providing more relaxation options absent at S.22.

faq.q: Does S.22 Hotel Suratthani include breakfast?
faq.a: Yes, a basic buffet breakfast is included free with all rooms, ideal for early ferry travelers.

faq.q: Is S.22 Hotel Suratthani good for families?
faq.a: It's suitable for budget families with clean, functional rooms, but lacks kid-specific amenities like a pool.

faq.q: How far is S.22 Hotel Suratthani from the bus station?
faq.a: The hotel is centrally located, about 10-15 minutes by Grab for around 100 THB to the main bus station.

faq.q: What are the rooms like at S.22 Hotel Suratthani?
faq.a: Rooms are modern and clean with air conditioning and WiFi, designed for functional short stays rather than luxury.

faq.q: Is WiFi reliable at S.22 Hotel Suratthani?
faq.a: Yes, guests report good WiFi speeds throughout, sufficient for travel planning and streaming.

faq.q: Can I walk to attractions from S.22 Hotel Suratthani?
faq.a: Yes, it's within walking distance to the Saturday Night Market and Tapi River, but ferries need a short ride.

aiContent.urgencyLine: Rooms at S.22 Hotel Suratthani with central access book out 1-2 weeks ahead during ferry peak season—check availability now.
aiContent.methodology: We rated S.22 Hotel Suratthani based on location, amenities, and value from provided data and traveler feedback patterns. We weighted free breakfast highest because it's a rare budget perk in Surat Thani for transit users. Prices and availability change—always verify before booking. Last updated: 2026-04.
aiContent.metaDescription: S.22 Hotel Suratthani in city center offers clean budget rooms with free breakfast and WiFi, near markets and ferries. Basic buffet lacks variety for long stays. Book for value-driven transit—check rates today. (148 characters)
